heading: Breach of agreement: liability
label: 7699B Breach of agreement: liability
notes: Effective DateSecretary of Veterans Affairs to begin awarding scholarships under this subchapter for programs of study beginning no later than one year after , see , set out as a note under .Oct. 17, 2020section 502(c) of Pub. L. 116–171section 7698 of this title

children.text: A participant in the Program (other than a participant described in subsection (b)) who fails to accept payment, or instructs the educational institution in which the participant is enrolled not to accept payment, in whole or in part, of a scholarship under the agreement entered into under shall be liable to the United States for liquidated damages in the amount of $1,500.section 7604 of this title
Liability under paragraph (1) is in addition to any period of obligated service or other obligation or liability under such agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: The participant fails to maintain an acceptable level of academic standing in the educational institution in which the participant is enrolled (as determined by the educational institution under regulations prescribed by the Secretary).

children.label: B
children.text: The participant is dismissed from such educational institution for disciplinary reasons.

children.label: C
children.text: The participant voluntarily terminates the program of study in such educational institution before the completion of such program of study.
Liability under this subsection is in lieu of any service obligation arising under the agreement.

children.heading: .—Liability During Period of Obligated Service
children.label: c .—Liability During Period of Obligated Service

children.text: Except as provided in subsection (d), if a participant in the Program does not complete the period of obligated service of the participant, the United States shall be entitled to recover from the participant an amount determined in accordance with the following formula: A = 3Φ (t−s/t).

children.label: A
children.text: “A” is the amount the United States is entitled to recover.

children.label: i
children.text: the amounts paid under this subchapter to or on behalf of the participant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the interest on such amounts which would be payable if at the time the amounts were paid they were loans bearing interest at the maximum legal prevailing rate, as determined by the Treasurer of the United States.

children.label: C
children.text: “t” is the total number of months in the period of obligated service of the participant.

children.label: D
children.text: “s” is the number of months of such period served by the participant.

children.heading: .—Limitation on Liability for Reductions-in-force
children.label: d .—Limitation on Liability for Reductions-in-force
children.text: Liability shall not arise under subsection (c) if the participant fails to maintain employment as a Department employee due to a staffing adjustment.

children.heading: .—Period for Payment of Damages
children.label: e .—Period for Payment of Damages
children.text: Any amount of damages that the United States is entitled to recover under this section shall be paid to the United States within the one-year period beginning on the date of the breach of the agreement.
